page_metadata.biocontainers__summary: It is a public domain java image processing program, which was designed with an open architecture. custom acquisition, analysis and processing plugins can be developed using imagej’s built-in editor and a java compiler. user-written plugins make it possible to solve many image processing and analysis problems, from three-dimensional live-cell imaging, to radiological image processing, multiple imaging system data comparisons to automated hematology systems.
page_metadata.biotools__summary: It is a public domain Java image processing program, which was designed with an open architecture. Custom acquisition, analysis and processing plugins can be developed using ImageJ’s built-in editor and a Java compiler. User-written plugins make it possible to solve many image processing and analysis problems, from three-dimensional live-cell imaging, to radiological image processing, multiple imaging system data comparisons to automated hematology systems.
page_metadata.bioschemas__summary: It is a public domain Java image processing program, which was designed with an open architecture. Custom acquisition, analysis and processing plugins can be developed using ImageJ’s built-in editor and a Java compiler. User-written plugins make it possible to solve many image processing and analysis problems, from three-dimensional live-cell imaging, to radiological image processing, multiple imaging system data comparisons to automated hematology systems.
page_metadata.galaxy__summary: ImageJ2 is a new version of ImageJ for the next generation of multidimensionalimage data, with a focus on scientific imaging.

User-written plugins make it possible to solve many image processing and analysis problems, from three-dimensional live-cell imaging, to radiological image processing, multiple imaging system data comparisons to automated hematology systems.
